philosophy: My research is guided by a commitment to understanding how sustainable fashion can be made accessible, desirable, and culturally resonant for consumers. I work at the intersection of consumer behavior, design science, and textile innovation — using mixed-methods approaches to examine how sustainability is perceived, communicated, and adopted across diverse populations. I am particularly interested in natural and heritage fibers, zero-waste design, and the visual language of sustainable apparel. My work bridges empirical consumer research and creative design scholarship, with the goal of producing knowledge that is both academically rigorous and practically meaningful for the fashion industry.
